rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the intended component or implementation scope, following AADL architectural rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks at valid locations without modifying the structure
Use component type annexes for assumptions and guarantees that apply to the component type
Use implementation annexes for implementation-specific bindings and guarantees
Ensure all visible identifiers in the annex blocks are valid within the target scope
generic_rules: Maintain the existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Place annex blocks in the most specific scope that contains all referenced identifiers
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Use valid AGREE syntax and logical operators in annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic in AGREE contracts
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not place assumptions or guarantees in invalid scopes
